text_prompt.role: base semantic layer for every task
use_when: the brief is clear
direction should be explored first
no reliable reference is available
avoid_as_only_tool: the user already provided a strong reference

use_when: the scene is complex
composition should be retained
the user provided a scene sample
style is the main problem
a set of images must look unified
the user provided a style sample
avoid_as_only_tool: the face or outfit must stay the same
subject identity is the main issue

omni_reference.role: lock a person or object identity
use_when: the same character continues
the same product continues
cross-round consistency is needed
the user has stable long-term taste
the task can safely inherit personal taste
avoid_as_only_tool: the task must obey a narrow project style

use_when: brand visual direction is needed
series feel matters more than one-shot precision
the user provided a board of inspirations
avoid_as_only_tool: only one image needs a small fix
a single style sample is already enough

use_when: the user likes an image but cannot explain why
style cues should be extracted from an image
the prompt is too long
style words conflict
a compressed core expression is needed
prompt obedience matters more
the result should look more like a sheet or product shot
avoid_as_only_tool: the user explicitly wants stronger artistic polish

run_as_hd.role: finish a near-final result in higher resolution
use_when: direction is locked
only finish and clarity are missing
avoid_as_only_tool: subject and composition are still unstable

draft_mode.role: prototype directions faster at lower GPU cost
use_when: early exploration
proposal rounds
style creator previews
avoid_as_only_tool: when finish-level quality is already required

conversational_mode.role: turn natural-language ideation into draft prompts
use_when: the idea is still fuzzy
the user wants to talk through variations quickly
draft exploration is more important than final prompt stability
avoid_as_only_tool: the task is entering automation or template distillation

style_explorer.role: discover and shortlist reusable style codes
use_when: style direction is known but no style code is chosen yet
the user wants to browse community-proven sref options
avoid_as_only_tool: subject identity is the main issue
the task already has a fixed project style code

style_creator.role: distill reusable style codes for repeated future use
use_when: the user wants a reusable style code
a long-term style system should be built
avoid_as_only_tool: when the style direction is still unclear

editor.role: modify an existing image instead of rerunning a full generation
use_when: only part of the image should change
the canvas should be expanded
the overall direction is already correct
avoid_as_only_tool: the style direction is fundamentally wrong

vary_region.role: regenerate only a selected region inside an image
use_when: a local fix is needed
a new element should be added into one area

pan_zoom.role: expand the canvas or pull the camera back
use_when: more space is needed around the subject
the composition is too tight

smart_select.role: create cleaner irregular masks before erasing or replacing regions
use_when: the edit target has complex boundaries
background removal or background replacement is needed
avoid_as_only_tool: a simple rectangular edit is enough
the task is a full reroll

layers.role: place additional source images into a composition before regeneration
use_when: the user has extra source assets to position
image compositing should happen before regeneration
avoid_as_only_tool: the task only needs a local repaint
the whole image should be uniformly restyled

retexture.role: restyle an existing structure while keeping its composition
use_when: structure is right but the material or style is wrong
the whole image should be re-skinned without re-blocking it
avoid_as_only_tool: only one small area should change
the structural drawing is already wrong

text_generation.role: render short quoted words inside the image
use_when: short titles or signs are needed
the text is a visible part of the scene

video_generation.role: animate a starting frame into a short video clip
use_when: a still image is already approved
short motion output is required
avoid_as_only_tool: when the keyframe is still unstable

repeat_batch.role: run the same prompt multiple times for batch ideation
use_when: parallel option generation is needed
the prompt is already clean and stable

seed_lock.role: support controlled A/B tests and seed-locked reruns
use_when: prompt experiments should be compared cleanly
a seed-locked HD rerun should stay on the same branch
avoid_as_only_tool: subject consistency is the real goal

routing_rules.reference_strategy: start with style, do not lock identity too early
keep freedom high and avoid locking too early
style unification comes before scene semantics
use image semantics first for complex space
prioritize shape accuracy and commercial finish
project-level unity comes before single-image precision
lock the subject first, then apply only requested changes
determine what the reference is for before anything else
keep the current structure and modify only the selected region or canvas bounds
anchor motion to a stable starting frame and avoid image-chain references
separate style discovery from delivery prompts and distill reusable style codes
